---
title: "Home"
---

# GeneWeave

Welcome to the **GeneWeave** documentation. GeneWeave automatically queries **ten public databases** to enrich a gene list and produces ready-to-use reports (HTML, CSV, Excel, JSON).

> **GeneWeave** = *gene* + *weave* — weaving together annotations from NCBI, Ensembl, UniProt, and more into one unified table.

## Purpose

From a text file containing `GENE,organism` pairs, the tool:

1. **Queries** NCBI, Ensembl, UniProt, STRING, ProSite, Pfam, PDB, KEGG, and Gene Ontology;
2. **Consolidates** identifiers, links, and annotations into a single table;
3. **Generates** reports with coverage statistics;
4. **Caches** results to speed up subsequent runs.

## Quick start

### From source (Python)

```bash
# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Annotate the sample file (12 genes)
python3 main.py data/GeneSymbols_2.txt

# Browse results in your browser
python3 viewer.py --open
```

Output files are written to the `results/` folder by default.

### Desktop binary (no Python)

Build or download a release for your OS — see [Desktop App](desktop.qmd):

```bash
# Linux example after extract
./GeneWeave
```

## Documentation structure

| Page | Content |
|------|---------|
| [Prerequisites](prerequisites.qmd) | Environment, dependencies, configuration |
| [Usage](usage.qmd) | Command line, GUI, web viewer |
| [Desktop App](desktop.qmd) | Standalone Linux / Windows binaries |
| [How It Works](how-it-works.qmd) | Pipeline, databases, architecture |
| [Output Files](output-files.qmd) | Output formats and interpretation |

## Databases queried

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Genomic reference
- **NCBI** — gene IDs, RefSeq transcripts, proteins
- **Ensembl** — IDs, genome browser, orthologs

### Proteins and domains
- **UniProt** — accession and official name
- **ProSite** — functional motifs
- **Pfam** — protein domains

### Networks and pathways
- **STRING** — protein–protein interactions
- **KEGG** — IDs and metabolic pathways
- **Gene Ontology (GO)** — molecular function, biological process, cellular component

### 3D structure
- **PDB** — crystallographic structures

:::

## Overview diagram

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[GENE,organism file] --> B[main.py]
    B --> C[NCBI / Ensembl]
    C --> D[UniProt]
    D --> E[ProSite / Pfam / GO]
    B --> F[STRING / KEGG / PDB]
    E --> G[Reports]
    F --> G
    G --> H[results/]
    G --> I[Local cache]
    H --> J[viewer.py]
```

Build this documentation:

```bash
python3 docs/render.py
```

This starts an HTTP preview server and opens your default browser.
